surface.name: AssemblyAI Quickstart through a first pre-recorded transcription
surface.selection_basis: The official Quickstart says it teaches developers to transcribe and analyze an audio file and ends with a working script that transcribes a hosted sample in one SDK call. It labels the printed sample transcript as the whole first call.

alternatives_considered.name: AssemblyAI Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Quickstart presents the Playground as a no-code try-first alternative, while the SDK route is the documented API integration path.

alternatives_considered.name: JavaScript SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer implementation of the same first call; Python appears first and has the shortest published command sequence.

alternatives_considered.name: HTTP API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Quickstart directs non-SDK users to HTTP after demonstrating that the SDK handles upload, submission, and polling in one call.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an AssemblyAI account and use the official Python Quickstart to transcribe the hosted wildfire sample until transcript text is printed.
assumed_prior_state: No AssemblyAI account, project, API key, billing method, SDK, script, transcript, or prior API usage is assumed.
Python, pip, a shell, and outbound access to the hosted sample are available; no signup, key creation, package installation, API request, credit use, or transcription was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Not established end to end by public official documentation
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: No complete first-user route can be established because the required email-registration or Google-authentication transitions after the initial signup choice are not publicly enumerated.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The public signup response renders only Create your account, Continue with Google, and Continue with email; it does not expose the subsequent identity fields, verification actions, or account-completion state.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The required account-registration transition is not established by AssemblyAI's public official documentation.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart later identifies printed transcript text as the whole first call, but a route beginning with no account cannot reach the documented dashboard key steps without inventing hidden signup transitions.

prerequisites.requirement: Create an AssemblyAI account through the signup page's Google or email route; the downstream registration states are not publicly enumerated.
Create a descriptive, project-scoped API key under Workspace, Manage, API Keys in the dashboard.
Have Python and pip to install the assemblyai package and run the published script.
The limited free trial includes pre-recorded transcription and $50 of audio processing; adding a credit card is documented as the upgrade action, not a first-call prerequisite.

primary_path.action: Open the AssemblyAI signup page linked by the Quickstart's dashboard requirement.
primary_path.output: A Create your account page offers Continue with Google and Continue with email.
primary_path.success_signal: Both supported account-entry choices are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The page also links existing users to Sign in.

primary_path.action: Select Continue with email to begin a direct account-registration route.
primary_path.output: A client-side registration continuation is required.
primary_path.success_signal: The initial signup choice has been made.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The public response and official docs do not enumerate the email fields, validation, verification, terms, submit action, or completed-account destination that follow.

candidate_path_gap.reason: After the undocumented signup transition, the official continuation is to log in, open Workspace, Manage, API Keys, select Create New API Key, enter a descriptive name, select Create, export it as ASSEMBLYAI_API_KEY, run `pip install assemblyai`, save the published Python script as `transcribe.py`, and run `python transcribe.py`. The SDK uploads, submits, and polls in one call, and the terminal prints the wildfire transcript text.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The detailed post-account path cannot form an end-to-end first-user candidate while the mandatory registration transition remains hidden.

branches.path: Select Continue with Google and complete the provider route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the hidden email-registration transition with an undocumented external identity-provider transition.

branches.path: Install with `npm install assemblyai`, save the published JavaScript as `transcribe.js`, and run `node transcribe.js`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the SDK and runtime but converges on printed transcript text for the same hosted audio.

branches.path: Pass a local file path instead of the hosted URL; the SDK uploads it.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds local-file selection and upload while preserving the same transcription terminal.

friction_gates.description: The required email or Google signup continuation is not publicly enumerated after the first choice.
API access requires a project-scoped key created and retrieved from the authenticated dashboard.
The transcription call includes submission and polling, though the SDK performs these actions within the single call.

sources.title: AssemblyAI | Sign Up
evidence_supported: Absence of rendered downstream registration states in the public response

sources.title: Can I sign up for free? | AssemblyAI Frequently Asked Questions
sections_used: Can I sign up for free?
What happens when you exceed the free tier limit?

uncertainties.question: What exact email fields, password rules, terms, verification actions, Google consent states, and post-registration screens currently lead to the AssemblyAI dashboard?
uncertainties.impact: The mandatory account portion of the first mile cannot be reconstructed or counted.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The signup page renders only the first provider choice and the Quickstart assumes a dashboard and API key.

excluded_after_success: No complete first-success boundary is claimed while account registration remains undocumented.

Speaker labels, language detection, local files, complete-example options, transcript retention operations, streaming transcription, Speech Understanding, billing upgrades, and production scaling occur after the documented first printed transcript.
